course_title: Physiology and Pharmacology of Human Disease
course_description:
An integrated pathophysiology and related pharmacology in human disease relevant to allied health sciences. Students will be introduced to the physiology of major systems (nervous, respiratory, vascular, endocrine) and how the process of ageing can disrupt these systems and lead to disease. Students will be introduced to common medicinal drugs used in the treatment of conditions mentioned in this course.

BIOM2015 Physiology and Pharmacology of Human Disease provides a comprehensive overview of integrated pathophysiology and related pharmacology in human disease relevant to allied health sciences.ﾠIt involves three (3) lectures per week, seven (7) practical sessions across the semester, and communication task presented in written or video format about a disease topic relevant to the concepts covered in the course.

course_contacts.name: Student and Academic Administration Team
course_contacts.notes: The SBMS Student and Academic Administration Team is located on Level 1 of the Sir William Macgregor Building (64-130).

timetable:
The timetable for this course is available on the
UQ Public Timetable
.
Additional timetable information
Three of the practical sessions occur in the Integrated Pathology Learning Centre (IPLC) at Herston campus and will require students to travel to this location. These practicals are directly related to the communication task completed over the semester.
course_aims: Students undertaking BIOM2015 should understand and appreciate that:

learning_outcomes.description: identify and explain the structure and function of physiological systems
analyse and describe how physiological systems function in health and disease
explain the regulation and integration of physiological systems
describe the effects of ageing on physiological systems in health and disease
integrate the mechanisms of drug actions and physiological systems in disease states
explore the practical application of human pathophysiology and explain the theoretical basis for experimental outcomes
effectively communicate complex biological concepts in health and disease orally and/or in written form to a range of audiences.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 1 To Week 2 (22 Jul - 04 Aug)
learning_activities.topic: Endocrine 1-5 [Colthorpe] Thyroid function/dysfunction & the control of metabolism. Glucose homeostasis & diabetes. Endocrine control of growth & growth abnormalities; Osteoporosis.
Course introduction [Kibedi] Course introduction to aims, learning objectives, learning activities and assessment
NO PRACTICALS

Group 1 Multimedia Communication Assignment [Kibedi] Overview and explanation of multimedia assignment; topic selection. This practical is scheduled to occur in the Integrated Pathology Learning Centre at Herston campus and will require you to travel to Herston.

Group 2 Multimedia Communication Assignment [Kibedi] Overview and explanation of multimedia assignment; topic selection. This practical is scheduled to occur in the Integrated Pathology Learning Centre at Herston campus and will require you to travel to Herston.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 3 To Week 4 (05 Aug - 18 Aug)
learning_activities.topic: CVS 1-4 [Chunduri] Structure, function and disease of the Cardiovascular System.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 4 To Week 6 (12 Aug - 01 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Respiratory Physiology 1-5 [Burgess] Structure, function and disease of the Respiratory System.
Group 1 ECG, Blood Pressure & Heart Sounds
Group 2 ECG, Blood Pressure & Heart Sounds
NO PRACTICAL

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 6 To Week 7 (26 Aug - 08 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Autonomic Nervous System & Special Senses 1-4 [Spiller] Structure & function of the ANS Structure, function & diseases of vision, hearing/balance & touch/pain
Group 1 Oral Glucose Tolerance Test
Group 2 Oral Glucose Tolerance Test
NO LECTURE There will be no lecture on Thursday of Week 7. We encourage you to use this time to rest, study or prepare assessment.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 8 To Week 9 (09 Sep - 22 Sep)
learning_activities.topic: Central Nervous System 1-5 [Manchadi] CNS overview structure and function. Learning and memory, and associated disorders. Age-related dementia and Alzheimer s disease, Alzheimer s disease related pharmacology. Nutrition and aging in dementia, prevention strategies of age-related dementia.

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 10 To Week 11 (30 Sep - 13 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: GIT 1-5 [Kibedi] Ulcerative Colitis and Chron's Disease Effects of nutrition and aging on IBD and IBS Bowel cancer Fatty liver disease
Group 1 Parkinson's Disease [Manchadi]
Group 2 Parkinson's Disease [Manchadi]
NO LECTURE (PUBLIC HOLIDAY)

learning_activities.learning_period: Multiple weeks From Week 12 To Week 13 (14 Oct - 27 Oct)
learning_activities.topic: Renal System 1-4 [Ainscough] Water balance dehydration in aging Acid-base balance Chronic kidney disease, dialysis and relevant pharmacology Urinary incontinence and related pharmacology
Group 1 Special Senses [Spiller]
Group 2 Special Senses [Spiller]
Inspera practise run & Revision Inspera practise run & Revision
FREE LECTURE SLOT [TBC]
